name: Chocolate Shortbread Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Cookies:
1. In a double boiler, melt semisweet chocolate over hot (not simmering) water. Set aside to cool to lukewarm.
2. Cream butter in a mixing bowl until smooth.
3. Beat in melted chocolate, vanilla, flour, powdered sugar, cocoa powder, and salt to form a dough. Wrap dough in plastic wrap.
4. Chill for 30 minutes or until firm.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Roll dough into 1-inch balls, then roll each ball into a thick log.
7. Place logs on an ungreased cookie sheet.
8. Press dough with the tines of a fork to form cookies about 1/4 inch thick. Keep them oval in shape.
9. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until set. Be careful not to overbake. Cool on wire racks.
Dipping chocolates:
1. In a small bowl set over hot water, melt white chocolate with 1/2 cup cream. Stir until smooth. Keep the bowl over the water so the chocolate stays smooth and melted.
2. Melt semisweet chocolate with the remaining 1/2 cup cream in another pan of hot water. Stir until smooth.
3. Keep the chocolate warm.
4. Dip one end of each cookie into white chocolate, then the other end into semisweet chocolate.
5. Place cookies on cooling racks to let the chocolate set. Repeat with all cookies.
